summary: Frontend-only attendance review completed with source code validation and live deployed probe. Requested attendance logic appears correctly implemented in code, but end-to-end role-based runtime verification is blocked in this environment.

ui_bugs.issue: Rendered controls are not reliably queryable via Playwright selectors in this runtime (canvas/semantics limitation), blocking deterministic click/assert flows.
ui_bugs.selector: role/text/css selectors on deployed Flutter web

integration_issues.flow: Attendance role-based validations (captain vs vice vs player)
integration_issues.issue: Cannot execute full runtime verification because /app/memory/test_credentials.md has no persisted credentials and Flutter SDK is unavailable for integration_test execution.

action_items: Run Flutter integration test on attendance flows with real captain/vice/player accounts (environment currently lacks Flutter SDK and test credentials).
Add stable test hooks (Flutter Keys/semantics labels) to attendance filter widgets and hero completion badge to improve automated verification.
Main agent should self-verify captain-only visibility and filtering behavior on deployed app using authenticated accounts.
critical_code_review_comments: Captain-only filter visibility is correctly gated by activeViewer?.isCaptain == true (vice/non-captain do not see it).
Captain filters are collapsed by default (isCaptainFiltersExpanded initialized false).
Requested filters are implemented as 3 independent fields (nome/cognome/id console), applied with AND logic.
No separate 'in attesa' captain filter exists in the reviewed implementation.
Hero card green/completed state is driven by all selected-day summaries having pendingCount == 0 (for manager summary context).
Potential testability gap: new attendance filter inputs and collapse toggle have no explicit test keys/semantics labels.
test_credentials: Missing: /app/memory/test_credentials.md states no persisted test credentials.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Live probe against https://squadra-web.onrender.com loads correctly, but Playwright still cannot reliably target Flutter-rendered controls in this container. Prioritize Flutter integration_test execution with valid captain/vice/player credentials.
rca of the issue: Primary blocker RCA: environment lacks Flutter SDK (flutter command not found), and deployed Flutter web control nodes are not consistently machine-selectable for Playwright role/text assertions. This prevents conclusive runtime verification of role-based attendance filters and hero-state transitions despite positive source-level implementation checks.
